title: Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act
short_title: Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act
plain_summary: This bill lets a group called the National Medal of Honor Museum Foundation build a monument on the National Mall in Washington DC. The monument will honor people who received the Medal of Honor, which is the highest military award for bravery. The bill is named after Hershel Woody Williams, who was the last living person from World War Two to have this special medal before he died in 2022. The monument will be built in an important area near famous landmarks like the Lincoln Memorial.
last_action_text: Committee on Energy and Natural Resources Subcommittee on National Parks. Hearings held.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress states these facts. In two thousand twenty one, Congress unanimously passed and the President signed a law that allowed the National Medal of Honor Museum Foundation to build a monument on federal land in Washington DC. This monument would honor the brave acts and sacrifices of Medal of Honor recipients. President Abraham Lincoln created the Medal of Honor one hundred sixty years ago on March twenty fifth eighteen sixty three. The first Medal of Honor was awarded on that same date. In nineteen ninety one, President George H W Bush made March twenty fifth National Medal of Honor Day every year. The Medal of Honor is given to members of all military branches as the highest award for bravery in combat. The number of living Medal of Honor recipients keeps getting smaller, which makes it more important to honor their heroic sacrifices and inspire future generations. Hershel Woody Williams was the last World War Two Medal of Honor recipient and died in two thousand twenty two. His body was placed in honor at the United States Capitol. The Medal of Honor represents the best of our nation. It goes to ordinary citizens who took extraordinary action beyond what was required of them and became heroes of our country. These examples inspire all citizens and spark the true spirit of America within us. The Medal of Honor is important enough at the national and historical level to deserve a place on the National Mall. Placing the National Medal of Honor Monument close to the Lincoln Memorial within the Reserve would respectfully extend Lincoln's lasting legacy and recognize what ordinary people can accomplish when working for the greater good.

sections.title: Section 3 National Medal of Honor Monument Location
sections.text: The monument must be built in a specific location. Despite what another law says about where monuments can be placed, this Medal of Honor monument that was authorized by an earlier law must be located within the Reserve. The Reserve is the area around the National Mall that includes the most important monuments in Washington DC. All other rules about building commemorative monuments still apply to this project, except for the location requirement that is changed by this section.
